This works perfectly when I test using the SAP CAI Chat Preview. However, When i try to perform the same using the SAP CAI Web Client the user email seems to not captured within the "{{user.email}}" (I displayed the user mail onto the screen which seemed to display '' with the Web Client )

https://help.sap.com/docs/conversational-ai/concepts-of-sap-conversational-ai/runtime-data-accessible

Kindly let me know for any suggestions

entry.title: Re: Trigger event from SAP Document Management Service
entry.content:
CMIS provides a “change log” mechanism to allow applications to easily discover the set of changes that have occurred to objects stored in the repository since a previous point in time.
There a currently these change event types: created, updates, deleted, security. The repository must support the changeLogToken. Don't know, if SAP has implemented this, but you can test it by yourself, when creating a repository. You can consult the official documentation at oasis.org

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Can not find app - Edit Actual Costing Runs (CKMLCP)
entry.content:
Hi @wangxlbest,

Could you try this: Go to Maintain Business Users app (with administrator role), check your user's "Last Updated On" time, if it's not recent, try to navigate to the relevant role (e.g. inventory accountant) assigned to your user, delete your user from roles' assigned user and re-assign your user to this role. The assignment should be activated automatically and immediately after this. 

Then you can check your authorized apps again.

Thanks!

grace

entry.title: Inquiry on SAP Support Scope for EHP6 ERP 6 on New Hardware Platform Post-Maintenance
entry.content:
We are currently running our SAP landscape on the following stack:

 

  •  

  • SAP Application: SAP EHP6 for ERP 6.0

  •  

  • SAP Kernel: 721

  •  

  • Database: Oracle 11.2

  •  

  • Operating System: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12

  •  

  • Hardware Platform: Microsoft Hyper-V DIGITS R2 (now out of vendor maintenance)

 

We have come to understand that since our current hardware is out of maintenance, SAP may provide only limited support under such circumstances.

 

We are in the process of planning a migration to a new hardware platform while also upgrading our complete SAP stack. Before proceeding, we would like to get official clarification on the following points:

 

  1.  

  2. What are the exact limitations of SAP support when the underlying hardware is out of maintenance — specifically, which areas are covered and which are not?

  3.  

  4. Upon migration to a new supported hardware platform, what sort of full support is provided by SAP?

  5.  

  6. Are there any SAP Notes or guides that define SAP's support policy in such scenarios?

 

We are looking for this guidance to plan our risk assessment, migration strategy, and any necessary upgrades if required to maintain SAP support eligibility.

 

We appreciate your help in providing official clarity on the support coverage during this transition.

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry on SAP Support Scope for EHP6 ERP 6 on New Ha...
entry.content:
Here is the detailed information regarding your queries:

1. Exact Limitations of SAP Support When the Underlying Hardware is Out of Maintenance

When the underlying hardware is out of maintenance, SAP's support is limited in several key areas:

  • Issue Resolution: SAP may not be able to fully resolve issues that are directly related to the outdated hardware. This includes hardware-specific problems that cannot be replicated or tested on supported hardware.

  • Performance Optimization: SAP cannot guarantee optimal performance on hardware that is out of maintenance. Performance issues may arise due to incompatibilities or inefficiencies that are not present in supported hardware.

  • Security: Outdated hardware may not receive security updates from the hardware vendor, which can lead to vulnerabilities. SAP's ability to provide security-related support is limited in such cases.

  • Compatibility: There may be compatibility issues between the outdated hardware and newer versions of SAP software, operating systems, or databases. SAP cannot ensure compatibility or provide fixes for such issues.

  • Best Practices Guidance: SAP's best practices and recommendations are based on supported hardware configurations. When using outdated hardware, these guidelines may not be applicable or effective.

  • Vendor Collaboration: SAP collaborates with hardware vendors to resolve issues. When hardware is out of maintenance, this collaboration is limited, reducing the effectiveness of support.

2. Full Support Provided by SAP Upon Migration to a New Supported Hardware Platform

Upon migrating to a new supported hardware platform, SAP provides full support in the following areas:

  • Issue Resolution: SAP can fully address and resolve issues, including those related to hardware, as they can replicate and test on supported hardware.

  • Performance Optimization: SAP can provide guidance and support to ensure optimal performance on the new hardware platform.

  • Security: SAP can ensure that the hardware receives necessary security updates from the vendor, reducing vulnerabilities and providing security-related support.

  • Compatibility: SAP can ensure compatibility between the hardware and the latest versions of SAP software, operating systems, and databases, providing fixes for any issues that arise.

  • Best Practices Guidance: SAP can provide best practices and recommendations that are applicable and effective for the supported hardware configurations.

  • Vendor Collaboration: SAP can effectively collaborate with hardware vendors to resolve issues, ensuring comprehensive support.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter July 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*


SAP Datasphere Connectivity to Microsoft SQL Server

Our brand-new E-Learning session showcases all you need to know for creation of a connection between SAP Datasphere and Microsoft SQL server, including setup of a dataflow.

Learn more

 
More EGI Sessions for SAP Business Data Cloud

Our new Expert-guided Implementation workshops on SAP Business Data Cloud are now available in Portuguese and Spanish language versions, along with further sessions scheduled in English.

Check it out!

Strengthening Your SAP CPQ Deployment with Continuous Quality Checks

Boost your deployment readiness with SAP's continuous quality check (CQC) services! Ensure the seamless integration of SAP solutions by identifying and resolving issues early. Benefit from expert guidance, streamlined processes, and enhanced system performance. Get ready for a smoother deployment and maximize your business potential with SAP CQC.

Dive into the details now!

Refreshed E-learning for SAP Fiori

The latest SAP Fiori E-learning course "Update on SAP Fiori" is now live — bringing you a clear, practical overview of both available innovations and what’s on the roadmap for 2025. Whether you’re a UX lead, developer, or business user, this course will help you stay ahead of how SAP is reimagining user experience.

More in the blog post

Discover the Power of Parallel Line Within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Join our new E-learning course to get an introduction to the parallel line functionality with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Learn how to separate incremental configuration activities for seamless data migration, testing, and training. Dive into the three-system landscape and master key actions like triggering the rebase in the SAP CBC workspace.

Access the learning here

Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What’s New in Q2 2025

Exciting updates on the release navigator for SAP BTP! In the Q2 2025 update, we've expanded 11 new product pages, bringing you access to resources for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment and several SAP BTP security services. Dive into the details and explore each new page in our latest blog post!

Learn more

Unlock the Power of SAP HANA Cloud

Join our sessions or watch the recording to learn how to migrate from on-premise systems seamlessly. Discover benefits, best practices, and our unique value assurance support.

Full details

Accelerate and Simplify your Journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the Customer Evolution Kit

Still running your ECC or S/4HANA on-premise solutions? Join EvoKit for an interactive engagement that blends expert-led sessions, AI-powered tools, and tailored assessments — all designed to deliver personalized recommendations and unlock the full value of cloud transformation. Confidently plan and execute your move to the cloud with a program built to support every step.

Learn more and register here

2508 Early Release Series coming up from July 21 to July 25, 2025

During the series, we will host 21 live sessions designed by product experts for product experts to give customers, partners, and colleagues a special insider’s preview into the highlights for selected lines of business and industries, 1-2 weeks before the actual release. In a span of multiple days, we give you the opportunity to see the latest demos, ask your questions, and get answers straight from the experts before anyone else.
Here’s the blog post

SAP Concur, Your Enhanced Hub for Expense, Travel, and Invoice Resources

Welcome to a simpler, smarter way to manage your travel, expenses, and invoices with SAP Concur! The updated page now includes a wealth of helpful materials covering expense management, travel planning, and invoice processing. Whether you are new or experienced with SAP Concur, these resources will guide you through using the platform efficiently. These tools are crafted to help you save time, improve spend visibility, and ensure a smooth user experience from setup to daily use. Explore the SAP Concur page and take advantage of these additional resources to streamline your business processes. 

Click here to access (value maps access required)

Join Mohammed Ajouz, SAP’s SVP and Global Head of Technical Support, as he discusses with Oliver Huschke how SAP is moving beyond automation — and into a world of autonomous, intelligent agents. Highlights:

entry.title: Inventory Audit Report Shows Negative Cumulative Stock Value (SAP B1 10.0 FP2208)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I encountered an issue where the Inventory Audit Report for a specific item shows a negative Cumulative Value.

Below are the details of the scenario:

The item uses "Moving Average" valuation and currently has 0 stock.

1). When running the Inventory Audit Report for "All Warehouses", the cumulative stock value is 0.00.

2). However, when running the report for each warehouse individually:

Warehouse A shows a cumulative stock value of -10,000.

Warehouse B shows -5.

Warehouse C shows SAP Business ONE10,005.

The combined total of these values is 0, which matches the "All Warehouses" result.

Is this behavior considered normal?

Additional notes:

The system setting “Manage Item Cost per Warehouse” is not enabled.

SAP B1 version is "SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.201) FP 2208 (64-bit)".

Localization: UK International / Republic of Ireland.

Troubleshooting steps already taken:

Executed Inventory Revaluation to 0 the moving average cost.

Executed Restore Item Balances.

Followed the solution mentioned in this SAP Community thread, but the issue persists warehouse-specific audit reports still show individual values.

 Inventory Audit Shows Negative Cumulative Stock Value

Question:
How can I completely clear or zero out this item's stock value across all three warehouses?

Best regards,
Rita

entry.title: Re: 关于“工艺路线”导入模板无“报废”字段如何解决
entry.content:
Hi ZZ3987,

Please have a check in the following KBA:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2515151

> There are basically three distinct ways in which to approach this for Public Cloud based systems:

  1. Consult SAP Note 2999428 to find out which additional standard fields (if any) are offered by your desired migration object.
    • Additional standard fields are fields rarely used by customers for data migration. They are already available within the API and released by SAP to be used for data migration.
    • To no blow up the data migration objects with such rarely used fields, the customer that wants to make use of that fields can add them to the migration object by themselves
    • Details see SAP Note 2999428
  2. At SAP Online Help documentation about available migration objects, check whether or not your desired migration object supports custom fields via key user extensibility. In case it does, follow the "Custom Fields" section, within the MO's documentation on how to implement this.
    • Custom fields are self-defined additional fields for a specific business context 
  3. Finally, you can raise a feature request. For more details, see KBA 2676589 - Request for new features in SAP S/4HANA Data Migration Content.

Best regards, Vicky

entry.title: Re: Sales order stock determination on the delivery
entry.content: "If there is no special stock available during delivery creation, then, system will look for unrestricted batch stock. " - I don't think that is true. If it was, it would defeat the purpose of sales order special stock. If a sales order requires sales order special stock, it means it must not be delivered from anonymous stock or from another sales order special stock.

entry.title: Re: Storage bin determination for synchronous goods receipts
entry.content:
Hi,

The fact that it determines the GR-AREA means that it's not determining the correct warehouse process type from the start. In Public Cloud you have to create an entry in "Determine Warehouse Process Type". 

Make sure you create an entry (without document/item type) and Process Indicator "Receipt of (un)packed items from Vendor (synch goods movement)" . If you then link your new warehouse process type to it, system will no longer use the GR-AREA as default.

entry.title: Re: Creating new collective PR for multiple new component...
entry.content:
Hi All,

I had a similar requirement and after a lot of debug, the proposed solution was to store determined PR numbers (temporary # or already assigned) into a static table attribute of the BAdI class.

This would take place at the end of Function Module CO_ZF_BANF_PROC (Implicit Enhancement).

We later use that static table attribute to read the PR number (existing one or temporary/new #) during collective BAdI execution.

Best regards,

AF

entry.title: 🚀 SAP Cloud ALM: Interface Documentation Next Level! 🔍💡
entry.content:
🚀SAP Cloud ALM: Interface Documentation Next Level! 🔍💡

In the world of modern IT landscapes, interfaces are the backbone of every system architecture. Losing track of them means losing valuable time – and nerves. 😵💫 But fortunately, there's a real game-changer: SAP Cloud ALM offers a powerful feature for interface documentation that many still underestimate.

What's in it? 🔧📘

The interface documentation in SAP Cloud ALM is more than just a digital notebook. It takes structure, transparency, and team alignment to a new level. Here's an overview of the most important features:

🔹Central Documentation

All relevant interface information (APIs, endpoints, partner systems, protocols) in one place – accessible at any time.

🔹Live Linking

Linking interfaces with business processes, user stories, or system landscapes. This makes context visible! 🧠

🔹Cross-Technology Focus

Whether OData, IDoc, REST, or SOAP – SAP Cloud ALM knows them all. 💪

🔹Status Tracking & Ownership

Who is responsible? What's productive, what's still under development? Everything is clearly defined – no more black boxes! 🕵️♂️

🔹Integrated Collaboration

Comments, change history, automatic logs – the perfect foundation for DevOps and agile teams. 🤝💬

Why all this? 🎯

In complex transformation projects (S/4HANA, cloud integration, API-first strategies), clarity is worth its weight in gold. Well-maintained interface documentation isn't a "nice to have," but a true business enabler. It helps:

✅Find errors faster

✅Simplify onboarding

✅Ensure compliance

✅Accelerate innovation

Conclusion: More than just documentation – a true management tool! 🧭✨

SAP Cloud ALM brings a breath of fresh air to interface documentation – intuitive, connected, and future-oriented. Investing early here will save you a lot of effort later. So: Get out of the Excel spreadsheets and into the cloud! ☁️🔥

And here's the whole thing from practice for practical use:

The starting point is the "Libraries" app. You can display it via the Fiori catalog if you can't see the app.

Each interface can be assigned helpful attributes such as:

  • Title
  • Category / Interfaces Technology
  • Sending System
  • Middleware
  • Receiving System
  • Tags
  • Responsible Person

A bulk upload from an Excel list is also possible.

Linking interfaces to other elements, such as test cases, is very valuable.

#SAPCloudALM #InterfaceDocumentation #SAPIntegration #ALMRocks #DigitalTransformation #DevOps #S4HANA #ITGovernance #ProjectManagement #ProjectGovernance #Methods #Techniques #Tools #Technology #Quality #ApplicationLifecycleManagement #ALM #SAP #SAPCloudALM #CloudALM #SAPBTP #SAPActivate #AI #KI #Joule #SAPJoule #SAPBusinessTechnologyPlatform #BTP #SAPBTP 🚀💬

entry.title: Part 4 - Subcontracting with GST
entry.content:
Subcontracting Process with GST with Transactions

As per the GST model law the Subcontracting process can be run through two methods

  1. With Delivery Challan.
  2. Without Delivery Challan.

Refer to the SAP Notes https://ga.support.sap.com/dtp/viewer/#/tree/2257/actions/29115 for referring to the Prerequisite SAP Notes.

The below process is for subcontracting with Delivery challan. 

Configurations:

 Step 1:  Assign Sales Area to the Supplying Plant

A sales organization, a distribution channel and a division must be assigned to the plant from which the components are to be delivered to the Subcontracting Vendor.

Maintain the Sales org, Distribution Channel and Division using tcode SM30 for the view V_001W_IV.

Step 2: Maintain Delivery Type for Supplying Plant (For Outbound delivery):

 

Step 3:  Maintain Customer for the Vendor.

Create a BP and extend the BP as a Supplier and a Customer. Assign the customer number in the supplier. This would be required during the delivery challan creation as the delivery challan has to be created for a customer.

BP created and extended to a Supplier BP role.

BP 5100000044 extended as a Customer.

Assignment of Customer number into the Supplier

Step 4: Create a New Billing Document Types.

Tcode VOFA

Create new Billing types for the Outbound Subcontracting Challan and Subcontracting Invoice.

Billing type ZSN for subcontracting challan (You can create your own billing type) :

Billing Type ZSP for subcontracting invoice :

Step 5: Maintain the view J_1IG_V_SUBINV

Tcode SM30:

ZSN billing type is relevant for "Create Challan" and ZSP billing type is relevant for "Create Invoice", maintain both in the table  J_1IG_V_SUBINV table.

Step 6: Pricing Procedure-  Tcode OVKK

  • You can use an existing pricing procedure for the billing types created.
  • You may configure a separate pricing procedure as well.
  • Pricing procedure is determined based on Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division, Customer Pricing Procedure, Document Pricing Procedure.
  • Sales Organization, Distribution Channel and Division are derived from Sales area assigned to Supplying Plant.
  • Customer Pricing Procedure is fetched from the Customer maintained for Subcontracting vendor.
  • Document Pricing Procedure is fetched from Billing Document Types ZSN/ZSP.

Step 7: Set up ‘Delivery to Billing’ copy control.

 Tcode VTFA

  • Create copy control for the new Billing types(ZSP and ZSN) created in step 4, by copying the existing copy control setting of the Billing type ‘FX’ and Sales order type ‘OR’.
  • Save the settings.

 

Step 8: Create Finished Material Code

 

Step 9: Create Component Material Codes and the Sales View with Distribution Channel has to be extended for the components.

 

 

Step 10: Create Subcontracting Purchase Info Records.

 

Scenario Transaction:

Case 1a:  Tax code in PO when Tax is deductible tax and without Outbound Delivery with deductible tax code IP.

Tax codes and the value is maintained prior in the condition record against the deductible condition types.

Transaction Steps:

Step 1:  Stock of Component Materials in MB52 :

 

Step 2: Create Purchase Order With Item Category “ L”.

Explode Components-

Control Code of the material in the Purchase order: 

Tax code (Deductible Tax)

The values in the condition types JICG and JISG are getting populated on the basis of the condition record maintained in the FV11 Tcode and the rates are maintained in line with the condition table records. 

Step 3: Transfer Posting of Components to Subcontracting Vendor

MIGO -> Transfer Posting -> Purchase Order. Movement type 541 will be auto populated. 

Enter the Quantity, Item OK for both the components and click on Post. 

 

 

No Accounting Document generated at the time of Transfer Posting. 

Step 4: Stock check for components.

Tcode MB52 -

Individual stock check MMBE:

 

Step 5: Delivery challan creation

Tcode: J1igSubcon

Enter the Material Document Number generated at the time of Transfer Posting.

Select the components and click on Create Challan. 

Step 6:  VF03 Display Challan

Enter the challan no in the display billing documents screen.

Step 7:  MIGO of the Main Material

Accounting Entries during GRN:

Step 8:  MIRO

Accounting Entries:

Purchase Order History Update:

Step 9: Subcon Invoice Reconciliation.

Tcode J1igrecon, enter the MIGO document number and the year.

Click on Save. 

To cross check if reconciliation is done or not, we goto SE16N and the table J_1ig_subcon and enter the Challan number.

Status “F” signifies that it is fully reconciled.

Case 1b: Tax code in PO when Tax is Non-deductible tax without Outbound Delivery

Scenario Transaction Steps:

Step1:  Stock of Components

Step 2: Create Purchase Order With Item Category “ L”.

Non Deductible Tax Code selected.

Non Deductible Tax Codes in PO. JICN and JISN are getting populated based on the condition record maintained in the FV11 Condition record maintenance. .

Step 3: Transfer Posting of Components to Subcontracting Vendor

Perform Transfer Posting for both the line items.

No Accounting Document generated after Transfer Posting. 

Step 4: Individual Stock Check of components.

Tcode MMBE

Step 5: Delivery challan creation

Tcode J1igSubcon.

Enter the Material Document Number generated at the time of Transfer Posting, select the items and click on create challan. 

System would generate the challan number. 

Step 6: VF03 Display Challan

Step 7: MIGO of the Main Material

Accounting entries after GRN.

Step 8:  MIRO

Accounting Entries during MIRO, in this case the GST is not taken as an input credit. 

Updated PO History:

Step 9: Subcon Invoice Reconciliation. 

Tcode J1igrecon

Enter the GRN document number and the year.

Click on Save. 

To check if reconciliation is done or not, we goto SE16N and the table J_1ig_subcon and enter the challan number.

Case 2a: Tax code in PO when Tax is deductible tax with Outbound Delivery & Shipping.

Step1: Check Component Stock using MB52. 

Step 2: Create Purchase Order With Item Category “ L” with deductible tax code IP.

Tax codes and the value is maintained prior in the condition record against the deductible condition types.

Explode Components-

Tax code used for Deductible scenario, PO taxes shown below.

JICG and JISG are getting populated based on the condition record maintained in the FV11 Tcode.

Step 3: OBD creation for supplying components to Subcontracting Vendors

Prerequisites:

  • Transportation group & Loading group must be maintained for the component’s in the  Material Master’s General/Sales Data.
  • Shipping data with delivery priority & Shipping conditions to be maintained for the customer BP’s in the shipping tab.
  • Also the tax categories for the BP have to be maintained under the Billing Tab of the BP.

Outbound delivery creation’s Tcode ME2O

Enter the Supplier and the components provided.

Enter the Location –

Step 4: VL02N for Post Goods Issue

Enter the Outbound delivery created in the earlier step.

Step 5: Stock check for the individual components using MMBE.

Step 6: Delivery challan creation

Locate the Material Document number post after the PGI in the display outbound delivery

Enter the Tcode: J1igsubcon

Step 7: Display Challan

Step 8: Accounting Entries during Goods Receipt.

Step 9: MIRO

Accounting Entries during MIRO, the input GST taxes are credited. 

Step 10: Subcon Invoice reconciliation.

To check if reconciliation is done or not, we goto SE16N and the table J_1ig_subcon and enter the challan number.

Case 2b: Tax code in PO when Tax is Non-deductible tax with Outbound Delivery & Shipping.

Step1: Check Component Stock.

Step 2: Create Purchase Order With Item Category “ L” with Non Deductible Tax Code I0

Tax codes and the value is maintained prior in the condition record against the deductible condition types.

JICN and JISN are getting populated based on the condition record maintained in the FV11 Tcode.

Step 3: OBD creation for supplying components to Subcontracting Vendors.

Prerequisite:

  • Transportation group & Loading group must be maintained for the component’s in the  Material Master’s General/Sales Data.
  • Shipping data with delivery priority & Shipping conditions to be maintained for the customer BP’s in the shipping tab.
  • Also the tax categories for the BP have to be maintained under the Billing Tab of the BP.

OBD creation’s Tcode ME2O

Enter the Supplier and the components provided.

Step 4: VL02N for Post Goods Issue

Enter the Outbound delivery created in the earlier step.

Step 5: Stock check for the components

Step 6: Delivery challan creation  with Tcode J1igsubcon

Locate the Material Document number post after the PGI in the display outbound delivery

Step 7: Display Challan VF03. 

Step 8: Accounting Entries on Goods Receipt:

Step 9:  MIRO

Step 10: Subcon Invoice reconciliation

To check if reconciliation is done or not, we goto SE16N and the table J_1ig_subcon and enter the challan number.

Reference: help.sap.com. 

Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP 2508
entry.content:
Welcome to the second half of the year! I hope the next few months bring success, growth, and exciting opportunities!

Our team has been attentively considering your feedback and working hard to deliver new release content just for you. Whether you are managing complex software enhancements, aligning release schedules, or ensuring your projects run more efficiently, these updates are designed to keep you informed of the most recent features. In the upcoming 2508 release, we shall explore the compelling new content provided by SAP experts, now readily available to you.

As of today, content inside the release navigator will begin to be updated to 2508 through August! 

The release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP is a strategic tool designed to help businesses effectively manage and plan new software updates and releases. It serves as a guide for organizations to navigate the complexities of release cycles, providing essential information regarding new features, improvements, and changes in SAP products.

SAP Cloud ERP

Utilizing SAP Cloud ERP instead of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for SAP’s cloud-based ERP products is a strategic branding decision designed to simplify and enhance the message conveyed to customers and stakeholders. This change aims to establish a cohesive and easily recognizable brand identity that encapsulates the essence and capabilities of SAP’s innovative ERP offerings. By adopting the term "SAP Cloud ERP," SAP seeks to emphasize its comprehensive cloud solutions while reinforcing its commitment to delivering cutting-edge technologies that meet evolving business needs.

Moreover, this branding choice aligns with SAP's strategic vision for the future, positioning its cloud ERP offerings as central to its product lineup and as pivotal tools for digital transformation and business process optimization.  Ultimately, the use of this new branding language underscores SAP’s dedication to clarity, consistency, and customer centricity in all its communications and engagements within the global market. 

Newly Created and Upgraded Content

By providing a structured approach to managing releases, the release navigator empowers businesses to enhance their operational workflows, reduce downtime, and capitalize on new capabilities.

AI Content

  • The 2508 release of the SAP Cloud ERP includes new information and features related to Artificial Intelligence (AI). This update provides insights into how AI can be integrated into your SAP environment, offering advanced capabilities to enhance data processing, automate routine tasks, and deliver predictive analytics. By leveraging AI in this release, organizations can improve decision-making processes, optimize workflows, and gain deeper insights into business operations.

The release navigator will also contain information on the topics mentioned in the table below, offering a comprehensive overview that facilitates strategic planning and decision-making. This tool serves as a centralized resource for stakeholders across various lines of business, industries, and cross topics, ensuring they have access to the most relevant and up-to-date information concerning each domain.   

2025 Innovation Plan

Just a reminder, no tasks are required for the updates to the release navigator, as everything is automated. For more detailed information on product release dates, please refer to the Release Schedule card. Here you can find the UX innovation & content update plans for 2025.

Connect with Readiness@Scale 

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback or specific content requests for future updates to the release navigator. Please feel free contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post. 

To explore more about how the Readiness@Scale team is dedicated to overseeing release readiness within the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio, visit our Release Ready page.

Additionally, you can find all our team's blog posts by searching the tag  Readiness@Scale. Thank you for your continued involvement and support.

Best Run,  
Readiness@Scale  

Aurea Balderas

entry.title: Re: Getting error in SAP Integration Suite IFLOW - Unreso...
entry.content:
Hi Ramesh

   Finally i am able to resolve the issue. Now its working.

   This is my findings:

     In the Auth crendentials that we create in Security material

  1.    We need to select "Send as body parameter" for Client Authentication.
  2.    We need to select " application/x-www-form-urlencoded" for Content Type.

Gopal

entry.title: Cost Center Not Appearing for Balance Sheet Account in Depreciation Journal Entry – SAP B1 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have implemented Fixed Asset management in SAP Business One (HANA version) for a customer, and we’ve completed the configuration for depreciation posting. Everything is working fine, the depreciation run is generating journal entries as expected.

However, I’ve noticed that cost centers are only appearing for the P&L accounts (like Depreciation Expenses) in the journal entry. For the Balance Sheet side (like Accumulated Depreciation), the cost center field remains blank.

Here’s what I’ve verified:

  • In Chart of Accounts, the Balance Sheet account ("Accumulated Depreciation – Plant & Machinery") has ActType set to “Expenditure.”

  • The cost center is assigned at the asset master level.

  • Checked the box "Consolidate Journal Entry Rows by Distribution Rules" in Depreciation Run - Preview

I understand that SAP B1 only applies cost centers to P&L accounts, but since I’ve marked the BS account as “Expenditure” in ActType, I was expecting the cost center to reflect there as well.

Is there any way to force cost center assignment to Balance Sheet accounts in depreciation JE?
Or is this the standard behavior, regardless of the ActType setting?

I’ve attached a screenshot of the JE for reference (showing cost centers only on the expense lines).

 

Appreciate any insights or suggestions from those who’ve faced similar behavior.

Thanks in advance,
Sebastin

 

SAP Business One SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA

entry.title: SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction – July 2025 update
entry.content:
The SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction provides guidance to our SAP BusinessObjects customers on the product directions for SAP analytics solutions. We published a new version this month with some important updates which can be found here.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud

SAP Business Data Cloud delivers deep integration of Data Management, Analytics and Planning augmented by AI in one native cloud solution. This allows to move from insight to action, brings analytics closer to the point of decision and provides trusted insights from customers’ most valuable data.

SAP Analytics Cloud, as a key component of SAP Business Data Cloud, provides trustworthy analytics for any user from any context and remains the recommended option for all new planning and analytics projects. The closed loop from insight to action allows customers to identify challenges, analyze options and execute decisions from one place.

Our knowledge of customer data and integration to business process enable customers to rapidly deploy analytics to address their most important business problems.

 

SAP BusinessObjects BI

With more than 30 years of innovations, SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform (SAP BusinessObjects BI) equips thousands of companies which reliably manage enterprise reporting use cases at scale. Our revised maintenance strategy guarantees Mainstream Maintenance until at least end of 2031 which secures your investments on the long run, whether you want to deploy on premise or in a private cloud.

New maintenance strategy

Starting with BI 4.3, Mainstream Maintenance is now followed by Customer Specific Maintenance during which we deliver fixes for high and very high security issues (CVSS >= 7) during the first year. We are extending BI 4.3 Mainstream Maintenance until end of 2026 so that security fixes are guaranteed until end of 2027 as previously committed.

With SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, we adopt a revised delivery cycle which addresses the need for more stability while keeping regular updates: we are moving away from support packages delivered every year and supported only during 18 months to minor releases delivered every 2 years, benefiting from 3 years of Mainstream Maintenance, followed Customer Specific Maintenance including 1 year of security fixes.

We already plan for a next release, BI 2027, which should be available in Q1 2027, followed by another one 2 years later, and we will revisit our roadmap on a regular basis.

SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 and beyond

SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 is available since March 2025. This new version illustrates our strategy going forward: continue the modernization started in version 4.3 and streamline the suite by focusing on the core components while providing alternatives for unsupported ones.

Our strategy is based on 3 pillars: reach more business users, increase power users’ productivity and reduce total cost of ownership. To maximize the value for our customers, we will focus ongoing investments on the most-widely adopted SAP BusinessObjects BI solutions: SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence, single source .unx universes, SAP Crystal Reports, and the SAP BusinessObjects BI platform.

 SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office and SAP Lumira Designer will be maintained and supported, but no new feature will be delivered.

We will provide alternatives

With SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, we do not deliver anymore SAP Lumira Discovery, SAP Crystal Reports for Enterprise, SAP BusinessObjects Universe Design Tool and .unv universes, multi-source universes and associated connectivity, SAP BusinessObjects Analysis edition for OLAP, SAP BusinessObjects Live Office and SAP BusinessObjects Mobile, but we are recommending alternatives for all use cases.

We will stop supporting AIX and Solaris after version 4.3 and will focus on Windows and Linux.

 

Move your analytics to the cloud

For companies willing to move analytics workloads to the cloud, we offer 2 complementary options: SAP BusinessObjects BI operated in a private cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud.

SAP BusinessObjects, private cloud edition is an opportunity to shift from a maintenance contract to a subscription model while leveraging your existing investments in SAP BusinessObjects BI solutions.

SAP Business Data Cloud is recommended for new applications, use cases and users, and along with our partner community, we are committed to support every customer through this journey with tools and programs.

 

Want to learn more?

Enjoy right now the full SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction.

entry.title: Remove failed requests in Offline Mobile Development Kit app
entry.content:
Hi ,
I am currently developing an MDK application with Offline capabilities. For example, I can trigger a CreateEntity request which will create a request in backend. Once triggered, it saves locally and will be sent to backend on sync. But sometime the created entity might end up with error (may be made to fail due to business scenario). So, in that case the data will be still in local storage, which will anyway fail in subsequent syncs. Is there a way to remove those data if they fail from local storage, so that they will not be sent again in next syncs.

entry.title: 回复： SAP Analytics Cloud Experience Sharing: How to Batch Modify the Assignees of Calendar Processes
entry.content:
Hi, @JefB 

1、On the initialization of the script variables removeAssigneeFilterSelection & addAssigneeFilterSelection, namely, how to specify the contents in the dropdown boxes.

a. You need to obtain the list of user/team information in the system and then replicate it into the story application.

b. After selecting the story page, click on the “fx” icon beside it to edit the script, and select "onInitialization".

 

Input the following code into the script. It initializes the page for the new Assignee dropdown menu. The same logic applies to removeAssigneeFilterSelection.

var addAssigneeFilterID = tbl_CONTENT_LANE_1_Role.getDataSource().getDataSelections();
console.log(addAssigneeFilterID);
var selectedKeyAdd = "";
if (addAssigneeFilterID.length > 0) {
 	for (var j=0;j<addAssigneeFilterID.length; j++){		Dropdown_Add_Assignee.addItem(addAssigneeFilterID[j].USER_OR_TEAM,addAssigneeFilterID[j].DISPLAY_NAME);
		if (selectedKeyAdd === "" && j === 0) {
			selectedKeyAdd = addAssigneeFilterID[j].USER_OR_TEAM;
			Dropdown_Add_Assignee.setSelectedKey(selectedKeyAdd);
			//console.log(["selectedKey ", selectedKey]);
		}		
		//console.log(["CurrentMeasure ", measures]);	
	}	
}
AddAssigneeObject.setAddAssigneeFilter(selectedKeyAdd);

c. Add a script object named AddAssigneeObject to the story, and set up the method setAddAssigneeFilter. Then, implement this method by inputting the following code. Do the same for RemoveAssigneeObject.

tbl_CONTENT_LANE_1_Role.getDataSource().removeDimensionFilter("Account_BestRunJ_sold");
tbl_CONTENT_LANE_1_Role.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Account_BestRunJ_sold",selectedId);

Finally, the dropdown menu will be automatically populated when the page is viewed.

2、The question of whether the story app can be utilized by teams.

It's theoretically feasible since the system API enables populating user/team info from a table into a dropdown menu. If the table you import into the story includes team info, you can replace items on a team basis.

If you have any more questions, feel free to communicate with me.

entry.title: Understanding the Regulatory Change of eMPF Platform in Hong Kong
entry.content:
MPF in Hong Kong

The Mandatory Provident Fund (MPF) was introduced by the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) and launched in December 2000. It was provided to assist in the provision of retirement benefit for the workforce. MPF is an employment-based retirement protection system. Apart from exempt persons, employees (regular and casual) and self-employed persons who are aged 18 and under 65 are required to join an MPF scheme.

Employees and employers are both required under the MPF legislation to make mandatory contributions into the employee's MPF account, subject to the minimum and maximum relevant income levels. Employers and employees can also make voluntary contributions.

Generally, employers need to prepare a Remittance Statement (RS) to show the contribution amount that includes employee's relevant income, mandatory contributions and voluntary contributions made by both the employer and employee for each contribution period. Trustees shall follow the instruction on the RS allocate the contribution payment into the account of each employee. Currently, for each contribution period, employers are required to submit the RS to MPF trustees and make contributions for their employees on or before each contribution day.

What is the eMPF Platform?

The eMPF Platform is built and operated by the eMPF Platform Company Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Mandatory Provident Fund Schemes Authority (MPFA), to keep pace with the digital era and to facilitate future reform of the MPF system. The eMPF Platform centralizes and digitalizes all MPF schemes’ administrative processes, allowing users to manage MPF online at ease.

As of June 5, 2025, 7 MPF trustees and their schemes have onboarded the eMPF Platform. For the latest onboarding schedule, please refer to the official published eMPF Onboarding Schedule.

The eMPF Platform provides various electronic channels for employers to submit the RS. The electronic channels include:

  • Direct data input on the eMPF Platform
  • Upload of RS in the form of contribution data in the standardized data file
  • Upload of RS in the form of contribution data by standard API

SAP Solutions

SAP has released relevant enhancements to support customers in uploading standardized data files to the eMPF platform. The key requirements and scenarios supported for data submission are outlined as follows:

Specifications for submitting contribution data to the eMPF Platform - SAP Note 3578236

  • Extending the length of Member Class field from 10 characters to 50 characters.
  • Rendering the fields, Mobile Number and Email mandatory in the New Member Information section.
  • Removing the termination reasons, Wrong Enrolment (WRONG_ENROL) and Intra-group transfer (INTRA_GROUP_TRF) from the Termination of Member section.
  • Updating the allowed Reason for Termination for the Type L to "DEATH, DISMIS, CONTRACT_END, ILL_HEALTH, or RETIRE" for Long Service Payment/Severance Payment in the Termination of Member section.
  • Updating the relevant logic in the report HHKCPFE0
    •  In the Member Contribution Information section, if the employee is not entitled to the relevant contribution, the following fields should be left as blank (instead of user input as 0): Employer's Voluntary Contribution, Employer's Voluntary Contribution 2 and Employee's Voluntary Contribution.

Separate files for submitting MPF enrollment details for new hires - SAP Note 3491949

  • A new checkbox, Mark member's status as existing for new hire, is provided for an employee whose enrollment data file has been submitted before. As a result, in the generated contribution data file, the employee's status will be marked as 'E'.
  • A new checkbox, Separate enrollment details file, is provided to generate a separate enrollment data file that only includes new enrolment details for new members. 
  • A new checkbox, Hide new hires within 60 days, is provided for filtering out the new hires whose enrollment data are to be submitted offline. 

Generating MPF enrollment details for new hires without payroll results - SAP Note 3528292

  • Providing an option that allows users to generate a separate file of MPF enrollment details for new hires without including payroll results.

Option for generation of eMPF file by selection of specific provider - SAP Note 3545657

  • Providing an option that allows users to select the MPF trustees for generating MPF contribution data files.

Different employer IDs for different providers under same company for eMPF - SAP Note 3562657

  • If an employer has 2 MPF schemes, system enhances the return value starting with "A" of feature 27PFE. 
    • The new return value places the <Scheme Registration No.> after the <Employer Account No.>. The sequence is formatted as "A<Employer Account No.><Scheme Registration No.>"
    • The length of the <Employer Account No.> is 15 alphanumeric characters long, so the <Scheme Registration No.> starts from the 17th alphanumeric character.
    • The <Employer Account No.> is retrieved based on <Scheme Registration No.>

Generating eMPF contribution file filtered by MPF plan - SAP Note 3594955

  • The function Filter by MPF Plan is provided on the selection screen of program for generating eMPF contribution file.

Display maximum level of relevant income in eMPF contribution file - SAP Note 3595638

  • Adding a new return value "RX" in feature 27PFE that decides whether to display the maximum amount or the employee's relevant income when the employee's relevant income exceeds the maximum amount.

Get phone number and country code for mobile phone information - SAP Note 3599535

  • When the customer configures the return value as "Oxxxx" in the feature 27PFE, the report HHKCPFE0 gets both the telephone country code and mobile number from subtype xxxx of IT0105.
  • In addition, the customer can configure a default country code to auto-populate when no country codes are configured in the IT0105.
    • When the customer configures the return value as "Oxxxx/zzz" in feature 27PFE, the report reads the telephone country code as "zzz" once no country codes are configured in subtype xxxx.
    • When the customer configures the return value as "Oxxxx" and "Nyyyy/zzz" in feature 27PFE, the report reads the telephone country code as "zzz" once no country codes are configured in subtype yyyy.

Submission for case of termination without contributions - SAP Note 3605668

  • For new hires within 60 days of employment in which there are no contributions, the MPF report HHKCPFE0 will display the member's contribution information as blank, showing only general information and the termination details of the member.

For the latest updates regarding this regulatory change, please visit the link.

Terminology:

MPF - Mandatory Provident Fund
RS - Remittance Statement
MPFA - Provident Fund Schemes Authority


Resources and Support

For more detailed information of regulatory change released by SAP Globalization, and further collaboration and support on HCM localization for Greater China, please visit the provided links:

Regulatory Change Manager

Greater China - Human Capital Management Localization

 

Disclaimer:

SAP is not a law firm and is not able to provide any legal advice.

Dear @Myselfsantanu, 

Try creating a different check rule in the below path 
SPRO --> Financial supply chain management --> Credit management --> Credit risk monitoring --> Credit Limit check --> Define Checking rule

Copy any of the check rule and add the Step 130 (Check for Overdue Open Items) or 110 (Check for Age of Oldest Open Item) based on your required and enter the days in the parameter value inside the credit segment for each.

If multiple days are required created a new check rule for each criteria and assign it to the respective customer.

Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,

Surya.

entry.title: Tackling Taxes Complexity in Supplier Invoicing with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
In today’s global economy, supplier invoicing is more than just a financial transaction—it’s a compliance checkpoint. Governments around the world rely on taxes to fund public services, regulate trade, and drive economic growth. As a result, tax compliance in supplier invoicing has become a critical focus area for businesses.

 Why Tax Accuracy in Supplier Invoices Matters

Tax authorities are tightening controls to combat fraud and ensure accurate tax collection. This means that even minor errors in supplier invoices can lead to significant penalties, delayed payments, or strained supplier relationships.

To stay compliant and avoid costly mistakes, businesses must understand the key tax-related challenges that arise in supplier invoicing.

 Common Tax Challenges in Supplier Invoicing

Here are some of the most frequent issues companies face when processing supplier invoices:

  • Inconsistent Tax Rules: Tax definitions and calculation methods vary by country, region, and even industry. This makes it difficult to apply a one-size-fits-all approach to invoice validation.
  • Location-Based Tax Rates: Taxes often depend on the “Sold To” address, requiring accurate and up-to-date location data.
  • Commodity Code Dependencies: Different goods and services may be taxed differently based on their commodity codes, adding another layer of complexity.
  • Incomplete or Incorrect Tax Details: Supplier invoices may lack proper tax breakdowns or include incorrect tax codes, making it hard for buyers to validate them.
  • Country-Specific Tax Structures: Some countries apply multiple layers of taxes—such as VAT, sales tax, or excise duty—while others may include withholding taxes that reduce the payable amount.
  • Indirect vs. Withholding Taxes: Indirect taxes (e.g., VAT) are added to the invoice total, while withholding taxes are typically shown as deductions. Misclassifying these can lead to compliance issues.

The Role of Smart Invoicing Solutions

To deal with those concerns related to taxes and avoid unexpected fees and fines is very important for the customers to have smart invoicing solutions capable to verify the taxes applied in the header and in each line of the invoice and guarantee the correctness of all details.

To manage these challenges effectively, businesses need invoicing systems that can:

  • Validate Tax Details Automatically: Ensure that taxes are correctly applied at both the header and line-item levels of the invoice.
  • Adapt to Local Tax Regulations: Dynamically adjust tax calculations based on the supplier’s location and the nature of the goods or services.
  • Flag Discrepancies Early: Identify missing or incorrect tax information before the invoice is processed or paid.

 

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management Solution

The SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management solution helps customers to increase the number of invoices processed by the Accounts Payable team through automation and reducing payment processing costs on a per-invoice basis and overall.

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management let you receive invoices from different channels such as e-mail, manual uploads, SAP Business Network, the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solution (including invoices from Peppol network and governments), APIs, structured data, and third-party optical character recognition (OCR) partners.

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). It uses the SAP Document AI with AI and machine learning to enable OCR of paper invoices such as .pdf and .jpeg.

With the solution, you can:

  • Pay invoices on time and increase invoice management efficiency through intelligent technologies
  • Achieve greater automation in the invoice management process, freeing up resources for other activities
  • Ensure compliance to your business terms and agreements and automatically reject noncompliant or fraudulent activities

To know more about SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, please follow the link below:

 

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management @ SAP.com

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management & Right Taxes Determination

Since May 2025, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management can determine the right taxes amount in supplier invoices in two new ways:

  • Using an integrated External Tax Engine like Vertex or Thomson Reuters.  Later others SAP Partners will be added to this list of external engines.
  • Using an Internal Tax Engine supported with some new released tax tables

If the customer does not want to use those new taxes calculations procedures, can continue to deal with taxes in the invoices in the same way.

 How to Determine the Right Taxes Amount in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

To verify the taxes in an invoice, the user must simply click a button to start the taxes verification using the external or internal tax engine. 

In the May 2025 version of SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, there isn’t any automatic correction of taxes. There are only warnings related to the taxes that would be or not changed in the invoices by the user.

The Figure 1 below shows one invoice line item where there is a discrepancy from the tax sent from Supplier and the tax calculated in the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.   In this case only a warning appeared in the solution. The solution didn’t change the taxes values automatically.

 Figure 1 – Warning after tax calculation discrepancy

When using SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management integrated with an SAP Partner Tax Engine like Vertex, Thomson Reuters (or other Partners Engines in the future), most of the configurations are done in the external solution.

When the customer opts for using Internal Tax Tables engine will need to configure 3 tables in the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management:  Tax Code, Tax Type and Tax Rate.

Figure 2 – Basic Tax Configurations using Internal Tax Tables

 The Figure 3 below shows the process of verifying the taxes using the internal taxes tables engine built into SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.  In this case there is one invoice line item where there is a discrepancy from the tax sent from Supplier and the tax calculated in the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management solution.  

Figure 3 – Example of SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management using Internal Tables

Benefits achieved by the customers using SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management to determine the right taxes calculations

Using the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management integrated with an external tax engine from an SAP Partner or using the internal taxes calculation tables the customer can:   

  • Prevents tax overpayments or underpayments on invoices.
  • Mitigates taxes compliance risk especially with complex global tax laws.
  • Boosts efficiency, cutting Accounts Payable operational costs.
  • Improves accuracy by eliminating manual tax errors avoiding costly penalties.
  • Strengthens audit readiness, simplifying compliance reviews.

entry.title: SAC Transport - to overwrite objects, but not data
entry.content:
Hello,

I am transporting a solution using Content Network Storage.

For the first time, I need to transport everything from Prod to Dev. I chose "Overwrite objects and data", everything were transported except transactional data in models. Why data in models are not transported?

Later, I will need to transport new enhancements from Dev to Prod. In this case objects should be transported and overwritten, but not data. Surprisingly, SAC does not offer such option "Overwrite objects only" (not data). It means not only data in models, but also data in dimensions should not be overwritten too. What options do you choose for transporting from Dev to Prod?

Looking forward to hearing from you,
Oleksandr

entry.title: Side Effects in RAP Explained with Determination Example
entry.content:
In the world of SAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming), Determinations and Side Effects play a crucial role in delivering responsive and intelligent user experiences. While determinations are used to implement logic that reacts to changes in data during specific RAP phases (such as on modify or on save), side effects ensure that related fields or entities stay consistent and updated on the UI without additional user actions. 

In his blog, I will demonstrate a practical scenario where determinations are used to dynamically calculate values based on user input, and side effects are configured to immediately reflect these changes on the UI. This not only improves the data integrity but also enhances usability in Fiori applications. By the end of this post, you will have a clearer understanding of how to leverage determinations and side effects together effectively within a RAP-based application. 

DETERMINATION : 

  • Actions modifies the existing BO instance where as Determination modifies the existing BO instances based on some  trigger condition  
  • We can see that Actions also triggers based on the conditions but cannot modify the BO instances , we can be able to throw the error messages. 

Pre requisites : 

During modification we change/modify  some fields / requirements  and we can compute the values of  particular fields 

Determination can be called during  Transactional phase/Save sequence where as validations always called during the save sequence 

Once a determination  has been triggered , it must run independently from other determinations 

During determination result must change if we execute detrmination several time under same condition  

 

Scenario : 

Here the determination calculateTotalPrice which is defined for all the three entities 

(Travel, booking and booking supplements) handles the  calculation of total  price of one travel. 

Based on the booking Fee total price must be calculated for the travel  

 

SIDE-EFFECTS: 

  • The SAP Fiori UI does not necessarily trigger a reload of all  related BO after every User input , as it simply does not know anything about data changes  on the tables 
  • Side effects are  used to reload data, permissions or messages based on data changes in UI scenarios with draft enabled BO’s  
  • Side effects reloads the screen but it does not reload entire screen  
  •  It changes some of the field values or will enable/ disable some of the fied values    Or status of the data in transactional buffer --> based on these same things should appear in the front end  
  • We need to define side effects in Behaviour definition  
  • Side effects will only reload the fields in front end  ,nothing will happen in backend  
  • The data which is available in the backend it will reload in the front end whihc is not currently displaying properly  

 

Side effects have two parts : 

  • Triggering condition : specifies when you should reload ( can be actions / validations) 
  • Target : which fields should get  reloaded ( value of a field / multiple fields / reload entire entity ) 

 

Important points: 

  • Determinations calculates the total  price and updates it into the transcational buffer 

But in the front end screen older values will be there . 

 

Requirement : 

  • Whenever I change the booking fee and booking supplement data and flight price 
  • The total  price should be updated not only in transactional buffer but also in the front end automatically  

And whenever we are updating booking fee the reload of total price should happen  

Based on the trigger condition --> it should reload the values / fields  

 

Here the source --> Booking fee 

Target --> totalprice 

 

Syntax: 

Side effects { filed MyField affects Target } 

                         { $self affects Targets ; } 

                 { action MyAction affects Tragets ;} 

     { determine action MyDetermineAction  

    Executed on sources  

Affects Tagets } . 

 

In the target we can specify : 

  • $self : The current RAP BO is reloaded  
  • Entity _MyAssoc  : the specified associated entity  _MyAssoc is reloaded when the side effect is triggered  
  • Message  : all the messages in the response parameter reported are reloaded when the side effects is triggered  
  • We can  also als provide multiple targets  

 

Side effects { field MyField affects Field1 , Field2 , action Action1 , 

                        Action Action 2 [..] } 

 

RESTRICTIONS : 

  • Static actions  defined using the addition static must  not be used as a side effect trigger , nor as a side effect target  
  • In case of the trigger entity property $sefl , the target cannot be a field of the current entity $self itself ,  

However fields  from the associated entity can be defined using path  expressions  

 

Side Effect In BDL: 

  • Side effects can be specified exactly once for each RAP BO entity in the entity  

Behaviour definition  

  • Multiple side effects can  be summarized with curly brackets , separated by a semicolon (; ) , if required. 
  • The syntax ‘use side effects’ is specified in the behaviour definition header and all  side effects from all  RAP BO entities of the underlying RAP BO are reused 

 

1. Create db table for travel  

@EndUserText.label : 'travel table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zpd_dt_travel {
 
key client : abap.clnt not null; 
key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id not null; 
agency_id : /dmo/agency_id; 
customer_id : /dmo/customer_id; 
begin_date : /dmo/begin_date; 
end_date : /dmo/end_date; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zpd_dt_travel.currency_code' 
booking_fee : /dmo/booking_fee; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zpd_dt_travel.currency_code' 
total_price : /dmo/total_price; 
currency_code : /dmo/currency_code; 
description : /dmo/description; 
overall_status : /dmo/overall_status; 
created_by : abp_creation_user; 
created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl; 
last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user; 
last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 

}

2.Create db table for Booking

@EndUserText.label : 'tbale for booking details' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zpd_dt_booking { 

key client : abap.clnt not null; 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.label : 'Travel' 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false 
key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id not null 
with foreign key [0..*,1] zpd_dt_travel 
where travel_id = zpd_dt_booking.travel_id; 
key booking_id : /dmo/booking_id not null; 
booking_date : /dmo/booking_date; 
customer_id : /dmo/customer_id; 
carrier_id : /dmo/carrier_id; 
connection_id : /dmo/connection_id; 
flight_date : /dmo/flight_date; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zpd_dt_booking.currency_code' 
flight_price : /dmo/flight_price; 
currency_code : /dmo/currency_code; 
booking_status : /dmo/booking_status; 
last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 

}

 3.Crete a db table for Booking suppliment

@EndUserText.label : 'booking supplimnet' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zpd_dt_bsupplmnt { 

key client : abap.clnt not null; 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.label : 'Travel' 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false 
key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id not null 
with foreign key [0..*,1] zpd_dt_travel 
where travel_id = zpd_dt_bsupplmnt.travel_id; 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.label : 'Booking' 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false 
key booking_id : /dmo/booking_id not null 
with foreign key [0..*,1] zpd_dt_booking 
where travel_id = zpd_dt_bsupplmnt.travel_id 
and booking_id = zpd_dt_bsupplmnt.booking_id; 
key booking_supplement_id : /dmo/booking_supplement_id not null; 
supplement_id : /dmo/supplement_id; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zpd_dt_bsupplmnt.currency_code' 
price : /dmo/supplement_price; 
currency_code : /dmo/currency_code; 
last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 

}

 4.Create a root view entity for travel

AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'root view for travel' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 

} 

define root view entity zi_travel_detail as select from zpd_dt_travel 
composition [0..*] of ZI_BOOKING_DETAIL as _booking 
association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Agency as _agency on $projection.AgencyId = _agency.AgencyID  
association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Customer as _customer on $projection.CustomerId = _customer.CustomerID 
association [1..1] to I_Currency as _currency on $projection.CurrencyCode = _currency.Currency 
association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH as _status on $projection.OverallStatus = _status.OverallStatus 

{ 

key travel_id as TravelId, 
agency_id as AgencyId, 
customer_id as CustomerId, 
begin_date as BeginDate, 
end_date as EndDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
booking_fee as BookingFee, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
total_price as TotalPrice, 
currency_code as CurrencyCode, 
description as Description, 
overall_status as OverallStatus, 
@Semantics.user.createdBy: true 
created_by as CreatedBy, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true 
created_at as CreatedAt, 
@Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true 
last_changed_by as LastChangedBy, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt, 
_agency, 
_customer, 
_currency, 
_status, 
_booking 

}

5.Create interface view  for booking 

@EndUserText.label: 'root view for booking' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 

} 

define view entity ZI_BOOKING_DETAIL as select from zpd_dt_booking association to parent zi_travel_detail as _travel 
on $projection.TravelId = _travel.TravelId 
composition [0..*] of zi_booking_supp as _bookingsuppl  
association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Carrier as _carrier on $projection.CarrierId = _carrier.AirlineID 
association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Customer as _customer on $projection.CustomerId = _customer.CustomerID  
association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Connection as _connection on $projection.CarrierId = _connection.AirlineID 
and $projection.ConnectionId = _connection.ConnectionID 
association[1..1] to /DMO/I_Booking_Status_VH as _booking_status on $projection.BookingStatus = _booking_status.BookingStatus  

{  

key travel_id as TravelId, 
key booking_id as BookingId, 
booking_date as BookingDate, 
customer_id as CustomerId, 
carrier_id as CarrierId, 
connection_id as ConnectionId, 
flight_date as FlightDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
flight_price as FlightPrice, 
currency_code as CurrencyCode, 
booking_status as BookingStatus, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt, 
_carrier, 
_customer,
_connection,
_booking_status, 
_travel, 
_bookingsuppl 
}

6.Create Interface view for Booking suppliment

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'root view for booking suppliment' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 

define view entity zi_booking_supp as select from zpd_dt_bsupplmnt 
association to parent ZI_BOOKING_DETAIL as _booking on $projection.TravelId = _booking.TravelId 
and $projection.BookingId = _booking.BookingId 
association[1..1] to zi_travel_detail as _travel on $projection.TravelId = _travel.TravelId 
association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Supplement as _suppliment on $projection.SupplementId = _suppliment.SupplementID 
association[1..*] to /DMO/I_SupplementText as _supplimentText on $projection.SupplementId = _supplimentText.SupplementID  

{ 

key travel_id as TravelId,  
key booking_id as BookingId, 
key booking_supplement_id as BookingSupplementId, 
supplement_id as SupplementId, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
price as Price, 
currency_code as CurrencyCode, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt, 
_travel, 
_supplimentText, 
_suppliment, 
_booking 

}

7.Define projection for travel root entity 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for travel' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define root view entity zc_travel_det provider contract transactional_query 
as projection on zi_travel_detail 

{ 

key TravelId, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'AgencyName' ] 
AgencyId, 
_Agency.Name as AgencyName, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'CustomerName' ] 
CustomerId, 
_Customer.LastName as CustomerName, 
BeginDate, 
EndDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
BookingFee, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
TotalPrice, 
CurrencyCode, 
Description, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'OverallStatusText' ] 
OverallStatus, 
_Status._Text.Text as OverallStatusText : localized, 
CreatedBy, 
CreatedAt, 
LastChangedBy, 
LastChangedAt, 
/* Associations */ 
_Agency, 
_booking : redirected to composition child zc_booking_det, 
_Currency, 
_Customer, 
_Status 

}

 8.Define projection for booking  entity

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for booking' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define view entity zc_booking_det  
as projection on ZI_BOOKING_DETAIL 
{ 

key TravelId, 
key BookingId, 
BookingDate, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'CustomerName' ] 
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { 
name: '/DMO/I_Customer', 
element: 'CustomerID' 

} }] 

CustomerId, 
_customer.LastName as CustomerName, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'CarrierName' ] 
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { 
name: '/DMO/I_Carrier', 
element: 'AirlineId' 

} }] 

CarrierId, 
_carrier.Name as CarrierName, 
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { 
name: '/DMO/I_Flight', 
element: 'Connection Id' 

}  

}] 
ConnectionId,  
FlightDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode' 
FlightPrice, 
CurrencyCode, 
@ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'BookingStatusText' ] 
_booking_status._Text.Text as BookingStatusText : localized, 
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { 
name: '/DMO/I_Booking_Status_VH', 
element: 'Booking_Status' 

} }] 
BookingStatus, 
LastChangedAt, 
/* Associations */ 
_bookingsuppl : redirected to composition child zc_booking_supp, 
_booking_status, 
_carrier, 
_connection, 
_customer, 
_travel : redirected to parent zc_travel_det 

}

9.Define projection for booking  suppliment  entity

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for booking suppliment' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define view entity zc_booking_supp

14. Create behaviour definition travel root entity 

managed; 

strict ( 2 ); 
define behavior for zi_travel_detail //alias <alias_name> 
implementation in class zcl_bp_travel_maa unique 
persistent table zpd_dt_travel 
lock master 
authorization master ( global ) 
etag master LastChangedAt 
early numbering 

{ 

field ( readonly ) TravelId; 
action AcceptTravel result [1] $self; 
action RejectTravel result [1] $self; 

factory action CopyTravel [1]; 
internal action RecalcTotalPrice; 
determination CalcTotalPrice on modify { create; field BookingFee, CurrencyCode; } 

// authorization ( global ), 
create; 
update; 
delete; 
// field ( readonly ) TravelId; 
association _booking { create; } 
mapping for zpd_dt_travel 
{ 

TravelId = travel_id; 
AgencyId = agency_id; 
CustomerId = customer_id; 
BeginDate = begin_date; 
EndDate = end_date; 
BookingFee = booking_fee; 
TotalPrice = total_price; 
CurrencyCode = currency_code; 
Description = description; 
OverallStatus = overall_status; 
CreatedBy = created_by; 
CreatedAt = created_at; 
LastChangedBy = last_changed_by; 
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at; 

} 
} 
define behavior for ZI_BOOKING_DETAIL //alias <alias_name> 
implementation in class zcl_bp_booking_ma unique 
persistent table zpd_dt_booking 
lock dependent by _Travel 
//authorization dependent by _travel 
authorization master ( global ) 
etag master LastChangedAt 
early numbering 
{ 
update; 
delete; 
field ( readonly ) TravelId, BookingId;

determination CalcTotalPrice on modify { create; field FlightPrice, CurrencyCode; } 
association _travel; 
association _bookingsuppl { create; } 
mapping for zpd_dt_booking 

{ 
TravelId = travel_id; 
BookingId = booking_id; 
BookingDate = booking_date; 
CustomerId = customer_id; 
CarrierId = carrier_id; 
ConnectionId = connection_id; 
FlightDate = flight_date; 
FlightPrice = flight_price; 

CurrencyCode = currency_code; 
BookingStatus = booking_status; 
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at; 

} 

} 
define behavior for zi_booking_supp //alias <alias_name> 
implementation in class zcl_bp_booking_ma unique 
persistent table zpd_dt_bsupplmnt 
lock dependent by _Travel 
//authorization dependent by _Travel 
authorization master ( global ) 
etag master LastChangedAt 
early numbering 

{ 
update; 
delete; 
field ( readonly ) TravelId, BookingId, BookingSupplementId; 
determination CalcTotalPrice on modify { create; field Price, CurrencyCode; } 
association _Travel; 
association _Booking; 
mapping for zpd_dt_bsupplmnt 
{ 

SupplementId = supplement_id; 
TravelId = travel_id; 
BookingId = booking_id; 
BookingSupplementId = booking_supplement_id; 
Price = price; 
CurrencyCode = currency_code; 
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at; 

} 

}
